name: TitleMax Title Loans
description_short: The Tucson, AZ location of TitleMax Title Loans is at 6383 E 22nd St Ste. 121. Contact them at +1 520-917-0599.
description_long:
TitleMax Title Loans was founded on September 1, 1998, with its first location in Columbus, Georgia. The company is owned by Tracy Young and operated under parent company TMX Finance LLC (formerly TitleMax Holdings, LLC, renamed in 2010), headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. With over 900 to 1,100 store locations across approximately 16 states and more than 3,000 employees, TitleMax is one of the largest title lending companies in the United States. It is not BBB accredited, holds no CDFI, HUD, or NFCC certifications, and is not a nonprofit. It operates as a for-profit consumer lender licensed under state-specific regulations such as the Tennessee Flexible Credit Act and Tennessee Title Pledge Act.

TitleMax's core product is the vehicle title loan: customers use their paid-off or nearly paid-off car or motorcycle title as collateral to borrow up to $10,000 in cash, typically within one hour. The customer retains use of the vehicle during repayment. In states where title lending falls under pawn law (such as Georgia), TitleMax offers title pawns instead. The company also offers unsecured personal loans in select states. Loan costs are not disclosed upfront and vary heavily by state — documented APRs reach up to 264% in Tennessee and up to 300% for the first three months in Georgia, with general ranges reported between 100% and 300%+ APR. No standardized fee schedule is publicly listed nationally.

TitleMax's primary differentiator is speed and accessibility: loan decisions are made in approximately one hour, same-day cash is standard, and the company explicitly serves borrowers with poor or no credit who cannot qualify for traditional bank financing. It maintains a national footprint of physical stores, an online account portal at account.titlemax.com, and a mobile app (iOS confirmed, App Store ID 1376904382) that allows customers to make payments, check balances, and find store locations. TitleMax is also a NASCAR sponsorship partner, serving as an anchor sponsor of Front Row Motorsports through at least 2026.

The honest assessment: TitleMax fills a real gap for underbanked consumers who need fast, collateral-backed cash with no credit barrier. However, the triple-digit APRs make these loans extremely expensive — costs that are difficult to compare across states due to limited upfront disclosure. In 2016, parent company TMX Finance was fined $9 million by the federal government for misleading customers about full loan costs. TitleMax exited California entirely after AB 539 capped interest rates in 2020, signaling the company's business model depends on high-rate environments. These are high-risk, high-cost financial products best used only as a last resort.

pricing.guarantee_details: Not applicable. Title loans are loan products with no money-back guarantee.

pros: One of the largest title lenders in the US with 900–1,100+ store locations across ~16 states
Loan approval and cash disbursement typically completed within one hour
No credit check barrier — designed for borrowers with poor or no credit history
Customer keeps and uses their vehicle throughout the loan repayment period
Online account portal and mobile app available for payments, balance checks, and store finder
Offers both title loans and title pawns depending on state, plus personal loans in select states
Founded in 1998 with 25+ years of operational history in the title lending space
cons: APRs commonly range from 100% to 300%+, reaching up to 264% in Tennessee and 300% in Georgia — among the most expensive consumer loan products available
TMX Finance (parent company) was fined $9 million in 2016 by the federal government for misleading customers about full loan costs
Exited California entirely after the state's 2020 interest rate cap law (AB 539), indicating the business model is incompatible with consumer-protective APR ceilings
Loan costs and fee structures are not standardized or disclosed upfront nationally — consumers must inquire at the state/store level
Not BBB accredited; holds no consumer protection certifications (CDFI, NFCC, HUD)
best_for: Vehicle owners with a paid-off or nearly paid-off car or motorcycle who need same-day cash
Consumers with poor or no credit who are unable to qualify for bank loans or personal loans
Underbanked borrowers facing urgent short-term cash needs of up to $10,000
Borrowers in the ~16 states where TitleMax operates who need a physical storefront experience
diagnosis: TitleMax is best suited for vehicle-owning consumers with poor credit who face an urgent cash need and have exhausted lower-cost options. The main caveat is severe: APRs regularly exceed 200%, and the federal government has documented cases of cost misrepresentation by the parent company. These loans carry meaningful risk of a debt spiral and should be treated as a last resort, not a routine financial tool.
services: Car title loans (up to $10,000, vehicle title used as collateral)
Motorcycle title loans (motorcycle title used as collateral)
Title pawns (pawn-law states such as Georgia)
Personal loans (unsecured, available in select states)
Same-day cash disbursement (typically within one hour)
Mobile app for payments and account access (iOS confirmed)
In-store loan processing at 900–1,100+ physical locations
Store locator tool for finding nearest branch
